metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists anywhere in the record. Rouven Ehniss stated the requirement plainly on the 6 Mar 2026 call: 'we don't want to go now into the full transformation... we are looking for some big wins which we can present also as part of our digitalization strategy to say this is possible, this is how much it will cost us' - but no cost, cycle-time, headcount, or savings figure has been produced by either side across four months of Next_Step updates. The pain is qualitative only (unstructured PR/approval flows on their custom OA platform). Quantifying this is the single highest-leverage move to unblock the stalled budget approval.

champion.narrative: Rouven Ehniss (GM, Global Sourcing) is a developing champion: he has driven the process end-to-end (organizing the 6 Mar intro call, executing the NDA, issuing the proposal to management, and following up after the CPO's Horizon decline per the 2 Jul Next_Step), and explicitly told Zycus what leadership needs to say yes. What is still missing: no evidence he has secured or influenced the CPO directly, and he has stated he lacks unilateral budget authority. Hagan Brown has joined as a second WIK-side contact since 14 May 2026 (including a direct call with deal owner Carl Kimball on 22 May) but his role and internal influence are unconfirmed.

sources: Avoma call 2026-03-06 (Zycus intro pitch, reference customers)
competition.narrative: No rival vendor has been named by WIK in any call, field, or Next_Step entry across the 4-month cycle. The only competitor names in the record (SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP) appear in Zycus's own 6 Mar 2026 pitch describing wins at OTHER customers (WSA Audiology, Fairprice, ST Engineering) - not competitors WIK is evaluating. This reads as a sole-vendor evaluation; the live risk is inertia/no-decision (an unapproved budget and a disengaged CPO) rather than losing to a named rival.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is real and buyer-articulated. On the 6 Mar 2026 call, Kelvin Loh (Head of IT) described WIK's custom in-house OA platform as handling 'purchasing request... most of our purchasing requisition forms... all of the flows and all of the approvals,' flagged as a note pain point: the current OA system's scope is limited and needs integration to enable broader flows. Rouven Ehniss separately framed the organizational pain as needing 'quick wins' to justify a larger digitalization push rather than jumping to full transformation. Pain owner is IT (Kelvin Loh) operationally and Global Sourcing (Rouven Ehniss) strategically.

paper_process.narrative: Paper process has not started; the deal is still pre-commercial at the budget-approval stage. Josslyn Deng (Head of Legal Function) is mapped as an Opportunity Contact Role but has not appeared in any of the 5 discovered touchpoints, meaning legal has not yet been engaged directly. No MSA, Order Form, or SOW discussion appears in any Next_Step entry or call. Visibility here will only emerge once budget clears and contracting formally opens.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer is named or engaged. The CPO is referenced only by title in Next_Step__c (22 Jun 2026: 'CPO coming to Horizon - ANA proposal with Mgmt') and has never joined a single one of the 5 discovered touchpoints. On 2 Jul 2026 the CPO declined to attend the Horizon review where the proposal was to be discussed with leadership. Rouven Ehniss (GM, Global Sourcing) is the highest-engaged contact and told Zycus directly on the 6 Mar call that WIK needs 'buy-in of our board and of our organization' - i.e. he has stated he cannot approve the $150k spend himself. Budget authority sits above him, unmapped.

decision_process.narrative: The call sequence itself maps a clear process: initial discovery with the full buying group including IT (6 Mar), NDA execution and use-case submission (13-18 Mar), presales requirements-mapping sessions (14 Apr, 28 Apr), a process-alignment call (14 May), then a proposal issued to management for budget approval (6 Jun). The process has now stalled at the approval gate: the 22 Jun plan to review the proposal with leadership at Horizon fell through when the CPO declined (confirmed 2 Jul). No new process step or date has been set since. Approval authority and the review venue are the open questions.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are confirmed but narrow: WIK asked for two named AI use cases (Merlin Intake, ANA) to be assessed for feasibility, cost, and HK/China restrictions, and the 27 Apr 2026 Next_Step confirms 'decision to move forward based on requirements matching.' On the 6 Mar call, Kelvin Loh (Head of IT) tested integration fit against WIK's in-house custom OA platform and SSO via the relevant stakeholder, both of which were confirmed as feasible via the iSaaS middleware layer. What is still undefined: any formal weighting, an RFP/RFI structure, or a documented BRD - this remains an informal, requirements-matching evaluation rather than a structured scorecard process.

hard.ais_why:
• Per the Apr 28 2026 rep note from Guillaume Pasquet: 'Good clarification meeting on Sourcing for indirect full AI... Part of broader global digitalisation and strategic initiative as this Agentic AI use case will be the 1st one (Intake, ANA, Strategic Sourcing). Decision in Q2.' The buyer is explicitly framing the deal as their flagship Agentic AI rollout with three named Zycus AI products in mandatory scope.
• Per the Mar 06 2026 rep note: 'Interest in AI quick wins around negotiations. Use cases to be reviewed by presales. Need to check AI restriction in HK.' The buyer is actively probing for AI quick-win use cases and proactively managing HK regulatory AI restrictions - hallmarks of a customer treating AI as the primary selection criterion, not a side feature.
• High confidence: NextStep is unusually rich with AI-specific language across 8+ dated entries (Mar 06, 13, 18, 20, 27, Apr 13, 27, 28, May 11), products in scope are exclusively AI-led (iSource + Merlin Intake + ANA), and 'Delay in Agentic Sourcing may impact final decision' shows AI delivery is gating the close. Avoma fuzzy-matched to a different account (KPJ Health) so no transcript corroboration, but rep notes alone clear the bar.
